headline: Survey at 36% completion with strong regional spread but uneven respondent mix.
collection_status: 481 of 1,350 responses collected (35.6% of target). All 10 regions represented; LINDI, MARA, and TANGA account for 47% of current data. Pace suggests completion by Q2 2026 if acceleration maintained.
key_findings: Teacher engagement dominates: 260 KiuFunza teachers (54% of sample) plus 115 head teachers drive feedback; senior officials (DEO, ARC, REO) severely underrepresented at 16 responses total.
Expansion appetite is clear: 175 stakeholders (36%) prioritize expanding KiuFunza; 116 want new programs. Only 9 cite research as priority—misalignment with evidence-building needs.
Scale readiness gap critical: Average readiness score 2.8/5 contradicts 36% calling for expansion—suggests optimism on demand but concern on systems capacity.

Qualitative signals focus on sustainability: Feedback emphasizes government partnership, teacher incentives, digital monitoring, and policy integration—all prerequisite to scaling beyond current 260 teachers.
flags: District/regional education officials (DEO, ARC, REO, REO) total only 16 responses—critical for policy buy-in and scaling authority are undersampled.
Scale readiness (2.8/5) is well below threshold for expansion; risks poor quality if growth proceeds without systems strengthening first.
RUKWA (19), SINGIDA (28), SONGWE (29) lag significantly; risk regional insights lost if these don't reach ~100+ responses each.
recommendation: Urgently redirect collection toward district and regional officials (target: 80+ responses) to validate policy feasibility before promoting expansion narrative. Simultaneously investigate scale readiness barriers (likely training, monitoring capacity, incentives) to avoid scaling into capacity failure.
